name: Harvey Mason
abstract: Harvey William Mason (born February 22, 1947) is an American jazz drummer, record producer, and member of the band Fourplay. He was the original drummer for Herbie Hancock's band The Headhunters.

concerts.label: Gerry Mulligan, Chet Baker and Stan Getz

works.t: Babes in Arms: My Funny Valentine
There Will Never Be Another You

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: The drummers are Steve Gadd, Alex Acuña, Harvey Mason, Vic Firth playing tympani, and David Friedman playing xylophone, and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: for me, when I play with him live... Well, it reminds me that the last time I saw you play with Harvey was for Johnny Pate.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terri Lyne Carrington
about_quotes.evidence: When I listen to Herbie’s electric music from the seventies, the Head Hunter’s Band, Harvey Mason and Mike Clark, the way they played, it was a busier kind of funk...
about_quotes.doc_title: Terri Lyne Carrington interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 9, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I didn’t know he was a singer. It’s like Harvey Mason. Harvey Mason was percussion on all “The Cosby Show.” I didn’t know Harvey was a drummer then.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Harvey Mason, drummers like that you know, not only great percussion players but they’re very sensitive as to what the music is all about.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I think Harvey Mason was on that... He’s on it. Alex Acuña, Harvey Mason, Milt Holland, Abraham Laboriel, Lee Ritenour.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
